events.description: Chief of Branch of Realty H. M. Critchfield issued memorandum to Area Directors regarding requirement to cite applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in patent requests
Billings Area Office issued memorandum to all Superintendents regarding rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee, enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter
Bureau memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy for issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Land Operations Conference held in Aberdeen Area at Sioux City, Iowa, June 13-15, 1955
Committee appointed at Land Operations Conference on June 14, 1955, chaired by K. S. Engel
Committee made preliminary report to the conference on June 14
Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777 contained amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 deleting reference to deposit of fees for submarginal lands
Billings Area Office transmitted fee patent policy statement to superintendents
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents transmitting committee reports from Land Operations Conference in Aberdeen Area
Billings Area Office Branch of Realty transmitted copy of office memorandum of 11/22/55 re fee patent policy to Superintendent, North Cheyenne
Office memorandum issued regarding fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 16
Billings Area Office memorandum to Superintendents regarding disposition of revenue from submarginal lands, quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955
Central Office memorandum regarding disposition of submarginal land grazing fees and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Billings Area Office memorandum to all Superintendents and Officers in Charge regarding issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments

events.location: Pine Ridge, Rosebud, Crow Creek, Lower Brule Reservations
events.description: Range inventory survey made by Soil Conservation Service personnel under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; now considered obsolete due to radical change in concept of scientific range inventory
Rex Carey, Agency Soil Scientist, transferred from Standing Rock to Pine Ridge with headquarters at Martin
Statement on Bureau fee patent policy prepared and distributed to Area Directors
Bureau auditors called attention to failures to obtain payment for accrued irrigation charges or contract obligations in some land sale instances

financial_transactions.description: Grazing fees collected for submarginal land grazing privileges held in special deposit until further order under 25 CFR 71.27
Submarginal land grazing fees to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719, Rental of Land Not Otherwise Classified, per amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
Unpaid irrigation assessments including construction, operation and maintenance charges required to be collected at time of land sale per Act of March 7, 1928 and 25 CFR 151
Delinquent operation and maintenance charges must be deducted from land sale proceeds per bid form 5-114

financial_transactions.amount: 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee annually
financial_transactions.description: Recommended annual investment in conservation improvements equivalent to 20% of lease rental or permit grazing fee
Amortization schedule recommended for conservation improvements to facilitate reimbursement to lessee if lease is lost shortly after improvements placed on land

relationships.subject: Bureau of Indian Affairs
relationships.context: BLM called attention to issues with patent requests lacking citation of easement laws and approval dates

relationships.subject: Billings Area Office
relationships.context: Area office issuing directives to superintendents

relationships.subject: Soil Conservation Service
relationships.context: Conducted 1939 range surveys under T.C.B.I.A. agreement; proposed cooperation on soil surveys

relationships.subject: Indian women married to white men
relationships.context: Committee recommended declaring Indian women married to white men ineligible for grazing allocations as non-Indian husbands typically receive the subsidy

relationships.subject: Aberdeen Area
relationships.context: Approximately 6.5 million acres of Indian lands on reservations in North Dakota, South Dakota, and northeastern Nebraska

relationships.subject: Act of March 7, 1928
relationships.context: Creates first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects for unpaid costs

relationships.subject: 25 CFR 71.27
relationships.context: Regulation amended August 10, 1955 to remove requirement to hold fees in special deposit

correspondence.sender_title: Chief, Branch of Realty, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.recipient: Area Directors, Portland, Aberdeen, California, Billings, and Minneapolis
correspondence.subject: Request for issuance of patents in fee - requirement to cite easements and rights of way laws and approval dates
correspondence.action_requested: Specify applicable laws and approval dates for easements and rights of way in all patent requests to allow expeditious handling
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued; Billings Area Office forwarded to superintendents January 6, 1955

correspondence.subject: Rights of way involved in issuance of patents in fee
correspondence.action_requested: Realty staff to obtain information on rights of way and include in transmittals with patent applications or when Indian lands advertised for public sale are traversed by approved rights of way
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued enclosing copy of December 13, 1954 office letter

correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents in fee to competent applicants - clarification of policy
correspondence.action_requested: All Bureau employees processing or reviewing fee patent applications to be guided by policy in May 16 memorandum and clarifying statement; exceptions to be returned to Superintendent for further action
correspondence.outcome: Temporary manual release to be issued incorporating substance of memorandum and statement

correspondence.recipient: Superintendent and Officers in Charge, Billings Area
correspondence.subject: Disposition of revenue from submarginal lands
correspondence.action_requested: Funds held in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury General Fund Receipt Account 1719 before end of calendar year; future revenues to be deposited to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued quoting Central Office memorandum of November 23, 1955

correspondence.subject: Committee reports, Land Operations Conference, Aberdeen Area
correspondence.action_requested: Comments requested on conclusions reached by several committees at Land Operations Conference

correspondence.sender: Billings Area Office, Branch of Realty
correspondence.subject: Fee patent policy - copy of office memorandum of 11/22/55
correspondence.action_requested: For information - copy of office memorandum re fee patent policy set forth in memorandum of May 10 and subsequent policy statement transmitted June 3 and October 4, 1955
correspondence.outcome: Transmittal sent via regular mail for information

correspondence.recipient: All Superintendents and Officers in Charge
correspondence.subject: Issuance of patents and deeds subject to collection of unpaid irrigation assessments
correspondence.action_requested: Each Superintendent to establish satisfactory checking procedure to ensure compliance with irrigation assessment collection requirements before land sale transactions are finally completed
correspondence.outcome: Directive issued following audit findings of failures to collect accrued charges

correspondence.sender: Central Office, Bureau of Indian Affairs
correspondence.subject: Disposition of submarginal land grazing fees and amendment to 25 CFR 71.27
correspondence.action_requested: Funds in special deposits to be deposited to Treasury; future revenues to appropriate general fund revenue receipt account
correspondence.outcome: Forwarded by Billings Area Office to superintendents November 29, 1955

legislative_actions.outcome: Act of March 7, 1928 (45 Stat. 210) enacted, creating first lien against irrigable lands on Indian irrigation projects where construction, operation and maintenance costs remain unpaid, directing that such lien be recited in any patent or instrument issued for such lands

Amendment to 25 CFR 71.27 published in Federal Register Vol. 20, No. 155, page 5777, deleting reference to deposit of submarginal land grazing fees in special deposits; funds to be deposited to Treasury miscellaneous receipt account

Pending legislation to transfer submarginal lands and fees to respective tribes; not enacted as of memorandum date; provision could be made in legislation to credit funds on deposit in Treasury to tribe account
